title: Log Loss (Binary Cross-Entropy)
description:
Implement binary cross-entropy (log loss) for probabilistic classification.

`LogLoss = -1/N * sum(y * log(p) + (1-y) * log(1-p))`

where `y` is the true binary label and `p` is the predicted probability.

Lower is better. Perfect model: LogLoss→0. Random model on balanced data: LogLoss≈0.693.
constraints: Clip probabilities to [1e-15, 1-1e-15] to avoid log(0)
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def log_loss(y_true: np.ndarray, y_pred: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """Compute binary log loss.

    Args:
        y_true: True binary labels (0 or 1), shape (n,)
        y_pred: Predicted probabilities, shape (n,)

    Returns:
        Log loss as float (lower is better)
    """
    pass
explanation: Log loss directly penalizes confident wrong predictions more than uncertain ones. It is the negative log-likelihood of the data under the Bernoulli model defined by the predictions. It is the proper scoring rule for binary classification probabilities and is minimized by the true conditional probability.

test_cases.name: Near-perfect predictions near 0
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
result = log_loss(np.array([1,0,1]), np.array([0.999,0.001,0.999]))
assert result < 0.01

test_cases.name: Random predictions ~0.693
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
result = log_loss(np.array([1,0,1,0]), np.array([0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5]))
assert abs(result - 0.6931) < 0.01

test_cases.name: Result is positive
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
result = log_loss(np.array([1,0]), np.array([0.7,0.3]))
assert result > 0

test_cases.name: Confident wrong prediction has high loss
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
result_bad = log_loss(np.array([1]), np.array([0.01]))
result_good = log_loss(np.array([1]), np.array([0.99]))
assert result_bad > result_good
